title: Preview before update/delete
summary: Learn how to safely preview the rows affected by UPDATE and DELETE statements in SQL before making changes, using the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: Why preview before changing data?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you run an UPDATE or DELETE statement in SQL, you are making permanent changes to your data. Unlike SELECT, these changes can't be undone with a simple command. That's why it's a best practice to **preview** which rows will be affected before you run the actual UPDATE or DELETE.

For example, if you want to update the status of all test items in the `inventory_items` table, first run a SELECT with the same WHERE clause:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This shows you exactly which rows will be changed. Once you're sure, you can run:

```sql
UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'archived' WHERE is_test = 1;
```

Previewing helps prevent accidental data loss or unwanted changes.

sketchBlocks.title: Preview before deleting
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Deleting data is even riskier than updating it, because deleted rows are gone for good unless you have a backup. Always preview which rows will be deleted by running a SELECT statement with the same WHERE clause first.

For example, to delete all test items:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

If the results look correct, then run:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This habit helps you avoid deleting the wrong data by mistake.

quizDraft.title: Preview rows before updating
quizDraft.prompt: You want to update the status of all items in the 'Accessories' category to 'inactive'. Write a SQL statement to preview which rows will be affected.
quizDraft.hint: Use SELECT * with the same WHERE clause you would use in the UPDATE.

help.concept: Previewing affected rows with SELECT helps you see which data will change before running an UPDATE.
help.hint_1: Write a SELECT statement that filters for category = 'Accessories'.
help.hint_2: Use SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Accessories';

quizDraft.title: Preview rows before deleting
quizDraft.prompt: Before deleting all test items (where is_test = 1), write a SQL statement to preview which rows will be deleted.
quizDraft.hint: Use SELECT to see the rows matching is_test = 1.

help.concept: Always preview rows with SELECT before running a DELETE to avoid accidental data loss.
help.hint_1: Use SELECT * and filter for is_test = 1.
help.hint_2: Try SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;

quizDraft.title: Update after previewing
quizDraft.prompt: After previewing, update all items in the 'Kitchen' category to set their status to 'inactive'. Write the SQL UPDATE statement.
quizDraft.hint: Use UPDATE with SET and a WHERE clause for category = 'Kitchen'.

help.concept: After previewing with SELECT, use UPDATE with the same WHERE clause to safely change only the intended rows.
help.hint_1: UPDATE inventory_items SET status = ... WHERE category = 'Kitchen'.
help.hint_2: Remember to set status to 'inactive' and filter for category = 'Kitchen'.

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'inactive' WHERE category = 'Kitchen';

quizDraft.title: Why is previewing before UPDATE or DELETE important?
quizDraft.prompt: Select all reasons why previewing affected rows before running UPDATE or DELETE is important.
quizDraft.hint: Think about risks and benefits of previewing.

help.concept: Previewing helps prevent mistakes and data loss by showing you which rows will be affected.
help.hint_1: Consider both data safety and error prevention.
help.hint_2: Previewing helps avoid accidental changes and lets you confirm your WHERE clause.

options.content: It helps you avoid accidentally changing or deleting the wrong rows.
It lets you confirm your WHERE clause is correct before making changes.
It automatically backs up your data.
It helps you see the exact data that will be affected.

quizDraft.title: Key clause for safe updates/deletes
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: To safely update or delete only specific rows, you should always include a _____ clause.
quizDraft.hint: This clause filters which rows are affected.

help.concept: The WHERE clause specifies which rows to update or delete, preventing changes to all rows.
help.hint_1: It's the clause that limits the affected rows.
help.hint_2: Without this clause, all rows would be updated or deleted.

quizDraft.template: To safely update or delete only specific rows, you should always include a _____ clause.
